posts.title: Graduate Salaries by Sector 2026
posts.caption:
What UK employers actually pay you in 2026.

We broke down graduate starting salaries across every major sector - from £22K in hospitality to £65K+ in finance and law.

The wild card? A supermarket grad scheme that beats most finance roles on base salary. Not what you expected.

Swipe to see where your sector lands.

posts.caption:

5 things to add to your CV right now

posts.title: UK Grad Job Market 2026
posts.caption:
The UK grad job market in 2026 is brutal. But most people are fighting the wrong battle.

140 applications per vacancy. 7% fewer jobs. Sounds bad.

But entry-level only dropped 6% while big schemes fell 10%. SMEs hire year-round. AI and tech are growing fastest.

The gaps exist. You just have to know where to look. Swipe.

internwise.co.uk

posts.title: London vs Regional Salary
posts.caption:
£33K in London vs £27K in Manchester.

Everyone assumes London wins. The maths says otherwise.

After rent (avg £1,500/mo) and travel (£180/mo), the London grad keeps £200 less every month than the Manchester grad.

The salary gap disappears in month one.

Do the maths before you default to London.

Find roles across the UK - internwise.co.uk

Sources: High Fliers Graduate Market Report 2026 (salaries) - Rightmove Rental Tracker Q1 2026 (rent) - TfL / Bee Network (transport costs)

posts.title: Turn Your Internship Into a Job Offer
posts.caption:
Most interns finish and leave. The ones who got return offers did 3 things differently.

It's not about being the smartest in the room. Not about staying late every night.

It's about treating the internship like a 10-week interview - and playing it accordingly.

Swipe for the full playbook.
